description: This scatter plot (Fig. 20) compares the product of tensile strength and elongation (strength-ductility product, GPa·%) versus density (g/cm³) for Be-bearing low-density steels against conventional and other lightweight steels. The x-axis ranges from 6.2 to 8.0 g/cm³ and the y-axis from 5 to 50 GPa·%. The Fe-30Mn-10Al-1.5C-xBe alloys (x = 0.5, 1.0, 1.5 wt%), marked by distinct symbols (square, circle, triangle, hexagon), occupy the upper-left region with densities of ~6.29–6.47 g/cm³ and strength-ductility products reaching ~46 GPa·% for the 0.5Be composition. These values significantly surpass conventional steels (40Cr, GCr15, 0Cr13, 1Cr23Ni18, Q235, etc.), which cluster at higher densities (7.6–7.9 g/cm³) with lower products (8–25 GPa·%). Other Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels (e.g., Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-3Cr, Fe-28Mn-9Al-1.2C-9Cr) plot at intermediate positions. A dashed curve suggests a performance envelope, with the 0.5Be steel approaching the optimum. The figure demonstrates that Be steels achieve a 21.38% density reduction compared to pure iron while maintaining exceptional mechanical properties, defining a new space in the density-strength-ductility relationship.
